title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Integration Workshop
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an MCP security test suite and operating policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""
